title: What a data contract guarantees
description: A data contract is an enforced agreement on schema, semantics, nullability, and freshness between a producer and its consumers, versioned and validated.
body: Use when datasets cross team or system boundaries. A contract specifies not just column names and types but value semantics (units, meaning), nullability, allowed ranges, and a freshness SLA, and it is versioned and enforced at the producer's publish step. It shifts breakage left: the producer's CI fails instead of a consumer's dashboard. Do not treat a shared table's current shape as an implicit contract. Actions: define and validate the contract at publish, version changes, and require sign-off on breaking ones. Validation signals include a publish-time contract check and a versioned contract file. Risk boundary: without an enforced contract, every producer change risks silently breaking unknown consumers.
